sources.notes: Statement and constraints: for every prefix of a binary string, count the k values allowing a partition into k substrings with equal beauty.
Detailed Chinese writeup derives the run-length-compressed recurrence for the contribution interval of each (k, cnt) pair.
Independent writeup starts from compressing the binary string into maximal runs and separating runs of length 1 from longer runs.
Additional solution source for Codeforces 2092F; used as a cross-check for the run-based counting approach.

annotation.summary: For each prefix of a binary string, count how many k allow splitting that prefix into exactly k substrings whose numbers of adjacent bit changes are all equal.
annotation.constraints: t up to 1e5, total n <= 1e6. The solution must be close to O(n log n) over one test file.
annotation.core_idea: Compress the string into maximal equal-character runs. Beauty is the number of run boundaries contained inside a substring, so a split with per-piece beauty c is controlled almost entirely by run indices; lengths matter only when a cut can be placed inside a run of length greater than one. The c=0 case is handled separately: every part must stay inside a run, contributing prefix_length - run_count + 1 possible k values. For c>0, enumerate the possible per-piece beauty c. Starting from the interval of run indices where one piece with beauty c can end, repeatedly append another piece. If the current boundary run has length greater than one, the next left boundary can be shifted by c runs; otherwise it must move by c+1 runs. Thus each fixed c contributes a sequence of intervals of prefix run indices, and adding these intervals with a difference array counts all k for every prefix. The total number of interval steps is harmonic, because for each c there are O(m/c) steps.
annotation.complexity: O(n log n) time and O(n) memory
tricks: Represent beauty by run-boundary count after run-length encoding.
Handle per-piece beauty 0 separately because pieces cannot cross any run boundary.
For c>0, each additional piece advances the feasible prefix-run interval by c or c+1 depending on whether an internal cut inside a run is possible.
Use a difference array over run indices to add all contribution intervals.

solution_variants.name: run-interval-contribution
solution_variants.summary: Compress the string into runs. Count c=0 directly, then enumerate positive per-piece beauty c and add feasible prefix-run intervals with a difference array.

tags.evidence: The solution first compresses the binary string into maximal runs, and all later feasibility logic is expressed in run indices.
The answer for each prefix is counted by how many run-index intervals are feasible for different equal-beauty values.
Each enumerated beauty value contributes to a whole interval of prefix run indices, and these contributions are accumulated by difference updates.
For per-piece beauty c, only O(m/c) intervals are processed, so the total work over all c is O(m log m).
The implementation adds +1/-1 on run-index intervals and later prefix-sums them to get the number of positive-beauty k values.
The algorithm still explicitly enumerates all positive per-piece beauty values and their interval steps, relying on the harmonic bound.
